title: Agentic Analysis for Agentic Infrastructure: An LLM-Powered Pipeline for Comparative Governance of DAO and Corporate AI Protocols
abstract: arXiv:2606.26203v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: As AI agent protocols proliferate, the governance structures shaping their interoperability standards remain empirically underexamined. We introduce an LLM-powered comparative pipeline for large-scale governance discourse analysis, integrating automated annotation, neural topic modeling, and multi-layer network analysis to study socio-technical power structures at scale. We validate it on two contrasting standards for agent interoperability: ERC-8004 (permissionless, on-chain) and Google A2A (corporate-led). Analyzing 4,323 governance participation records, we combine LLM-assisted coding, topic modeling, and multi-layer network analysis to examine how institutional design shapes thematic priorities and community structure. We find that while governance form influences substantive focus, both regimes exhibit comparable levels of participation inequality and community fragmentation. Discourse alignment is denser in the permissionless setti

translated_title: 面向代理基础设施的代理分析：一个基于LLM的DAO与公司AI协议比较治理流程
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26203v1 公告类型：新 摘要：随着AI代理协议不断涌现，塑造其互操作性标准的治理结构在实证层面仍缺乏深入研究。我们引入了一个基于大型语言模型的比较分析流程，用于大规模治理话语分析，整合了自动标注、神经主题建模和多层网络分析，以大规模研究社会技术权力结构。我们将其应用于两种对比鲜明的代理互操作性标准：ERC-8004（无许可、链上）和Google A2A（公司主导）。通过分析4,323条治理参与记录，我们结合了LLM辅助编码、主题建模和多层网络分析，考察制度设计如何塑造主题优先级和社区结构。我们发现，尽管治理形式影响实质性关注点，但两种制度在参与不平等和社区碎片化程度上表现相似。在无许可环境下，话语对齐更为紧密。
tldr: 本文提出了一种基于LLM的比较分析流程，通过自动标注、主题建模和多层网络分析，实证比较了无许可链上协议（ERC-8004）与公司主导协议（Google A2A）的治理结构，发现两者在参与不平等和社区碎片化上相似，但无许可环境下话语对齐更紧密。
en_tdlr: This paper introduces an LLM-powered comparative pipeline integrating automated annotation, topic modeling, and multi-layer network analysis to empirically compare governance structures of permissionless on-chain (ERC-8004) and corporate-led (Google A2A) protocols, finding similar levels of participation inequality and community fragmentation across both regimes, with denser discourse alignment in the permissionless setting.
